codeReview.summary: The feature meets the narrow deduplication checklist and adds focused unit coverage, but the contract is not safe for its stated role as an XCM receipt inbox. `receiveReceipt` is completely permissionless, so any account can spoof a receipt, consume a correlation ID, and permanently cause the legitimate receipt to revert as a duplicate.

issues.description: `receiveReceipt` has no origin or relay authentication. The first caller can submit arbitrary `correlationId`/`data`, set `processed[correlationId] = true`, and force the real XCM-delivered receipt to fail forever with `DuplicateCorrelationId`. That breaks the intended `incoming xcm execute -> XcmInbox` flow described in the mission plan and makes the inbox griefable/spoofable rather than a trustworthy receipt sink.

The test suite is strong on deduplication mechanics, but it only exercises arbitrary signer calls and never captures a trusted-origin requirement or spoofing attempt. Once the contract is fixed to authenticate the receipt source, add an authorization/regression test so this class of bug cannot silently return.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared state documents define XcmInbox as the sink for incoming XCM receipts and deduplication, but they never document who is authorized to call `receiveReceipt` or how that origin should be authenticated. That gap makes a permissionless inbox implementation an easy mistake.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/AGENTS.md:95-97` only says to add XcmInbox for correlation-ID tracking, while `/home/kpa/polkadot/M7_M8_EXECUTION_PLAN.md:236-238` says the contract should be triggered by incoming XCM execution and reject duplicates. The project library has `xcm-precompile.md` but no async receipt/origin-auth guidance.

summary: Reviewed the xcm-inbox-contract handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and the resulting Solidity/test files. Result: fail, because the inbox can be spoofed by any caller and therefore does not safely implement an actual XCM receipt sink despite otherwise meeting the narrow deduplication checklist.
